utilityName: City of Avondale Backflow and Cross-Connection Control
dueBasis: Avondale says annual testing is required, that customers will be notified when results are due, and that only a Certified Backflow Prevention Assembly Tester approved by the City may test devices in the system.
coveredPropertyTypes: Other city-water customers with required assemblies
officialListLabel: Open the Avondale approved tester list
penalties: Avondale refuses submission of test reports from companies that fail to maintain the required documentation and uses recognized testers for fire-protection and other protected services.
sourceExcerpt: Avondale is a strong Arizona utility because it publishes an annual-testing page, an approved tester list, and engineering standards that make fire protection, irrigation, and commercial backflow rules concrete.
verdictSummary: Avondale is a strong utility-first page because the city pairs annual testing, approved testers, and specific approved device classes in one public workflow.
whoIsAffected: Avondale water customers with required assemblies, especially commercial, multifamily, irrigation, and fire-protection services tied to city water.
residentialNotes: Single-family homes are not the main angle because Avondale's strongest public language sits around broader developments and protected services.
Residential irrigation still matters when the city has required an assembly and annual testing.
commercialNotes: Commercial intent is strong because Avondale explicitly says all developments except single-family residential require devices.
The approved tester list and city notification flow make this a real next-action utility page.

annualTesting.summary: Avondale requires annual testing of installed backflow prevention devices and notifies customers when test results become due.
highlights: Only a certified tester approved by the City of Avondale may test devices in the system.
Public Works staff file the test results and create the following year's schedule.
The city can refuse reports from testing companies that do not keep their documentation current.
workflowSteps: Check the Avondale due notice or current record.
Use a tester from the city-approved list.
Make sure the result is filed so the next year's testing cycle stays active.

irrigation.summary: Avondale's public rules fit irrigation because the city frames backpressure, chemical injectors, and cross-connections as drinking-water risks that require approved devices.
highlights: Annual testing applies once the device is installed.
Approved device classes are listed in the city manual.
The city-approved tester list gives irrigation customers a direct next action.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation assembly type Avondale expects.
Keep the test result and next-year schedule current with Public Works.

fireLine.summary: Avondale explicitly requires a double check valve assembly at all fire protection water connections.
highlights: Fire protection water connections are named directly in the engineering manual.
Certified testers recognized by Avondale must handle the annual and initial testing.
Device location and installation rules are spelled out in city standards.
workflowSteps: Confirm the service is a fire protection water connection.
Install the fire-line assembly per city detail.
Use an Avondale-approved tester for the acceptance and annual test cycle.
Confirm Avondale has required an assembly at the service.
Pick a company from the approved tester list.
Complete the annual or post-install test.
Get the result into the city file so the next schedule is generated correctly.
failureHighlights: Avondale publishes an approved tester list and annual-notice language.
Public Works manages both filing and next-year scheduling.
Fire protection water connections are explicitly regulated.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but Avondale narrows the provider path through its approved tester list.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost rises on fire-protection and larger commercial assemblies.
costBand.pricingNotes: The strongest local cost signal is staying inside the recognized-tester workflow so the city accepts the test.
